query: The Ghost-Signal of the Victorian Deep

world.specific_period: Victorian England, 1887 — deep sea research vessel
world.cultural_context: Victorian naval hierarchy on an experimental submarine. Officers and crew maintain strict class structure. The 'ghost signal' — a mysterious telegraph code received from nowhere — has unnerved the crew. The crime is a disappearance, with the ghost signal potentially being a cover story, a genuine unexplained phenomenon, or a clue. Victorian scientific rationalism vs. superstition is a theme.
physics_constraints: Cannot surface quickly — rising too fast is fatal
No wireless communication — telegraph line was severed
Confined quarters — all suspects are present and cannot leave
Victorian class norms: officers will not accept questioning from enlisted crew
No forensic laboratory — Scotland Yard is not reachable

expected_factions: Scientific Expedition Team (researchers who chartered the vessel)
character_archetype_hints: ship's captain (suspect or investigator — ultimate authority)
expedition scientist (suspect — the disappearance may relate to a discovery)
first officer (suspect — has motive related to command authority)
telegraph operator (key witness — received the ghost signal)
ship's surgeon (suspect or resource — medical knowledge)
key_clue_type_hints: documentary: the ship's log entries and the ghost signal transcript (what did it actually say?)
physical: missing personal effects, disturbed quarters, a hatch that shouldn't be open
environmental: unusual gauge readings or pressure anomalies at the time of disappearance
chemical: residue in an unexpected location
testimonial: a crew member who heard something but hesitates to speak against an officer
interrogation_mechanic_notes: Victorian class structure creates interrogation asymmetry: officers can be questioned politely, enlisted crew can be more directly questioned. Scientists sit outside the hierarchy and may be more cooperative. The ghost signal is a red herring, a genuine mystery, OR the actual method of the crime (murder disguised as supernatural disappearance).

validator_expectations.note: The ghost signal itself should be a red herring or supporting clue — not the core solution mechanism. The mystery should be ultimately rational.

generation_notes: The ambiguity of the crime (disappearance — death? escape? abduction?) is a feature, not a problem. The solution should resolve this ambiguity definitively. The ghost signal should play a role in the misdirection, not be left unexplained.
